summary: Keyword co-occurrence in minified bundle without corroborating behavior
details: A pattern match fired on the minified file extended/bulkLoad.min.d.js for co-occurrence of require("child_process"), the keyword GET, and hostname. This is a weak signal on a single-line minified bundle where unrelated identifiers commonly cluster on one line. There is no corroborating evidence of a hardcoded attacker C2 endpoint, no environment-variable scraping, no credential path access, and no install/import-time auto-execution path identified (no preinstall/postinstall hook involvement cited). The package is sort-btree, a B+tree data structure library, and the flagged file appears to be a minified bulk-loading helper. Without semantic verification of the bundled code's actual control flow, a public block would overclaim the signal. Routing to human review for de-minification and confirmation of intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
